details.title: AI Portfolio Intelligence Pilot
details.subtitle: A focused pilot to unify Monday.com, Power BI, and portfolio operating context into a trusted Q&A layer.
details.recommendation: Start with the $40K pilot build to prove trusted portfolio answers before expanding into AI agents and workflow automation.
executiveSummary: Triten has enough recurring reporting, lookup, and cross-system context-switching pain to justify a focused AI data pilot.
The strongest first move is not a broad automation program; it is a reliable operating foundation that answers high-value portfolio questions from governed sources.
The pilot is scoped to pay back on conservative year-one time savings while preserving larger avoided-hire and accounting-automation upside for later phases.
whatWeHeard: Teams lose time assembling investor-reporting context, extracting underwriting metrics, and keeping pipeline or inspection updates aligned across systems.
Leadership wants faster answers without creating another ungoverned reporting surface or relying on unsupported manual reconciliation.
Power BI and Monday.com are important existing surfaces, so the pilot should connect to current operating systems instead of replacing them.
details.investmentSummary: The recommended pilot is priced at $40K against an expected year-one value band of roughly $100K–$170K, with conservative payback under six months.
details.timelineSummary: The pilot is designed as a short, sequenced engagement: source mapping, data foundation, Q&A build, then QA and handoff.

scope.title: Source mapping and pilot question design
scope.description: Confirm the highest-value pilot questions, source owners, refresh expectations, and reconciliation checkpoints before building the AI layer.
deliverables: Source-system map for Power BI and Monday.com
outcomes: The pilot starts from concrete operating questions rather than generic AI demos.
Data access and ownership risks are surfaced before build work accelerates.

scope.title: Data foundation and ingestion
scope.description: Create the trusted data layer needed to answer portfolio and operating questions from current systems.
deliverables: Power BI and Monday.com ingestion path
outcomes: One governed foundation for pilot Q&A.
Reduced manual lookup and report assembly for recurring operating questions.

scope.title: MCP server and Q&A experience
scope.description: Build the pilot interface and tool layer that lets authorized users ask high-value questions over governed portfolio context.
deliverables: MCP server for approved pilot data access
Q&A workflow for priority operating questions
Prompt and response guardrails for pilot use cases
outcomes: Leadership and operators can get faster answers from trusted sources.
The team has a concrete foundation for later workflow agents.

scope.title: QA, documentation, and handoff
scope.description: Reconcile outputs, document the operating model, and hand off the pilot so Triten can evaluate the next phase confidently.
outcomes: The pilot is reviewed for accuracy before broader rollout.
The next phase is scoped from evidence instead of assumptions.

milestones.name: Kickoff and source map
outcomes: Confirm pilot questions, source owners, and access requirements.
Lock the data fields needed for the first Q&A workflows.

milestones.name: Data foundation build
outcomes: Connect and normalize priority Power BI and Monday.com context.
Validate reconciliation checks with Triten stakeholders.

milestones.name: Q&A layer and pilot workflows
outcomes: Deliver the MCP-backed pilot Q&A experience.
Test the highest-value investor reporting, underwriting, and pipeline questions.

milestones.name: QA, handoff, and Phase 2 plan
outcomes: Complete QA, documentation, and owner training.
Prioritize the next AI agent and workflow automation opportunities.

assumptions: Triten provides timely access to the relevant Power BI workspace, Monday.com boards, and source-system owners.
Pilot users are available for working sessions, data validation, and Q&A acceptance testing.
The pilot focuses on a bounded set of portfolio intelligence questions rather than every downstream accounting or reporting workflow.
exclusions: Full accounting automation and avoided-hire realization are deferred to a later phase.
Production rollout to all possible users is outside the pilot unless added through a change order.
New enterprise data warehouse procurement or major source-system replacement is not included.
clientInputs: Power BI workspace access and an owner for report/data model questions.
Monday.com board access and an owner for workflow/pipeline questions.
A prioritized list of pilot questions and acceptance criteria from the leadership team.
nextSteps: Approve the pilot investment and scope.
Schedule kickoff with source-system owners and pilot decision-makers.
Confirm data access, security expectations, and the initial pilot question backlog.
